Within the family Asparagaceae, Zeravschania ferulifolia is placed in the subfamily Agavoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics. intro: Zeravschania ferulifolia is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family, native to Central Asia and widely distributed in the mountainous regions of Tajikistan and Uzbekistan. Traditionally, Zeravschania ferulifolia has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
